description: Advertises the set of label-map regions available as IMAGE messages on a server. Each element describes one label — its human name, the IMAGE device that carries the label data, the integer pixel value that represents the label, a color, the spatial extent, and an optional owner-image reference.
rationale: A single IMAGE label-map can encode multiple labels (e.g. liver=1, kidney=2). LBMETA lets a server announce, per label, 'which pixel value is this named structure, what color should it render as, and which IMAGE do you fetch to get the pixels?' so a client can populate a segmentation list without downloading every IMAGE first.

fields.name: labels
name
fields.description: Human-readable label name or description (e.g. 'Liver'). Null-padded to 64 bytes.

fields.name: device_name
fields.description: Device-name suffix of the IMAGE message carrying the label-map pixels. A client retrieves that IMAGE via GET_IMAGE using this name.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Integer pixel value in the referenced IMAGE that corresponds to this label. Values are intended to be distinct within a single LBMETA element's referenced IMAGE, but that is not enforced at the wire level.

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Reserved; senders SHOULD write 0 and receivers MUST ignore.

fields.name: rgba
fields.description: Suggested rendering color as RGBA bytes. (0, 0, 0, 0) is legal and interpreted as 'no color preference'.

fields.name: size
fields.description: Extent of the label-map IMAGE in voxels: (Sx, Sy, Sz). Matches the referenced IMAGE's overall size.

fields.name: owner
fields.description: Optional device-name suffix of the IMAGE that this label is a segmentation of (the 'anatomy' image the label-map was derived from). May be empty (all null bytes) when not applicable.
Array of 116-byte LBMETA elements. Element count is derived as body_size / 116; a body whose size is not a multiple of 116 is malformed and MUST be rejected.

legacy_notes: Total body size is exactly 116 * N where N is the number of labels advertised. An empty LBMETA body (N=0, body_size=0) is legal and means 'no labels available'.

Multiple LBMETA elements MAY reference the same IMAGE (via device_name) with different `label` values; this is the intended way to advertise a multi-label IMAGE (e.g. liver=1 AND kidney=2 pointing to the same label-map IMAGE). Duplicate (device_name, label) pairs are undefined.

Upstream C++ library (at pinned SHA 94244fe) iterates `nelem` elements without verifying the body is large enough. A conformant implementation MUST reject any LBMETA message whose body_size is not a multiple of 116.
